sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, clicking the center cell reset ALL cells in the 3x3 grid back to gray! The entire grid is now gray. This is interesting - clicking the center cell seems to reset the board.
Also, I notice the right side of the bar has changed - it now shows only a small blue/sky blue segment at the top right, not the full 4x4 pattern.
So clicking the center cell resets the 3x3 grid. This means I need to be more careful about which cells I click.
Let me reconsider the game:
1. The left panel shows the target pattern (X pattern with 4 purple dots)
2. The 3x3 grid starts with some cells gray and some white
3. Clicking cells turns them green
4. The goal is to match the target pattern by turning the correct cells green
5. Clicking the center cell resets the board
So the target pattern is:
```
.  P  .
P  .  P
.  P  .
```
I need to click the 4 cells that correspond to the purple dots:
- Top-center (row 50, col 30)
- Middle-left (row 55, col 25)
- Middle-right (row 55, col 35)
- Bottom-center (row 60, col 30)
And NOT click the center cell (row 55, col 30) or the 4 corner cells.
Let me now click the 4 correct cells in sequence.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The 3x3 grid was reset. Now I need to click the 4 cells matching the X pattern:
# Top-center, Middle-left, Middle-right, Bottom-center
# From the ascii, the 3x3 grid cells are at:
# Top row: rows 49-51, cols 23-25 (left), 28-30 (center), 33-35 (right)
# Middle row: rows 54-56, cols 23-25 (left), 28-30 (center), 33-35 (right)
# Bottom row: rows 59-61, cols 23-25 (left), 28-30 (center), 33-35 (right)
# Target: top-center, middle-left, middle-right, bottom-center
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 29},  # top-center
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 24},  # middle-left
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 34},  # middle-right
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 29},  # bottom-center
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
